rulings.Case Title: YIBRAH TESFA VS. ANNE STAUBOLL et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 26, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTS ANNE STAUSBOLL, CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND MARCIA WIGINGTONYEE DEMURRER. (PART 1 OF 2) Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for violation of 42 U.S.C. § 12132 as against Defendants Stausboll and Wigingtonyee in their individual capacities. (See Alsbrook v. City of Maumelle (1999) 184 F.3d 999, 1005 n.8 ["Title II provides disabled individuals redress for discrimination by a 'public entity.' See 42 U.S.C. § 12132. That term, as it is defined within the statute, does not include individuals."].) The Demurrer is also sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action brought pursuant to 42 U.S.C. § 1983 as against Defendants CalPERS, Stausboll and Wigingtonyee to the extent the claims were asserted against them in their official capacities. States and state officers sued in their official capacity are immune from liability for claims brought under § 1983 pursuant to the 11th Amendment. (Catsouras v. Dept. of Cal. Highway Patrol (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 856, 892.) This ruling is not inconsistent with the Court's ruling on the first demurrer as the original Complaint named Defendant Stausboll only, and it was unclear whether she was being sued in her individual capacity, official capacity, or both. Thus, the determination of whether Defendant Stausboll was entitled to immunity was not apparent on the face of the Complaint. In contrast, here the Complaint clearly asserts the § 1983 claims against all three Defendants, and against the two individuals in both their official and individual capacities. Thus, the SAC clearly discloses that sovereign immunity is a bar to recovery against the Defendants in their official capacities. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the causes of action brought under § 1983 as to Defendants Stausboll and Wigingtonyee in their individual capacities. The allegations against Stausboll are mere conclusions and lack particularized facts demonstrating a constitutional deprivation. The Complaint fails to allege any wrongful conduct by Wigingtonyee. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all state law claims due to Plaintiff's failure to satisfy the claim presentation requirements of Gov. Code section 900 et seq. Plaintiff has made no showing or argument that any Defendants made any misrepresentations or concealed any material facts that Plaintiff relied on to decide not to file a claim. He points to nothing in the statements made by counsel during the writ proceedings that constituted an affirmative misrepresentation or concealment of material facts concerning the need for, or advisability of, a tort claim. Plaintiff's reliance on waiver, laches, equitable tolling, and continuing violations are all misplaced, and supported by no binding or persuasive authority. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief as there is no basis for Plaintiff's concern that Defendants may in the future stop paying his benefits, and Plaintiff is no longer a possible class representative. (tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD CARMIGNANI VS. SHAUNA LEA MATLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motoin To Compe Discovery Responses Establish Admissions, And Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 26, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT SHAUNA MATLIN Motion To Compel Discovery Responses Establish Admissions, And Impose Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Establish Admissions is DENIED. (CCP Section 2033.280(c). Parties are ORDERED to appear and be prepared to discuss whether the Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's discovery served on 11/21/14 as described in the Declaration of David C. Macpherson are complete, verified and without objection. SANCTIONS are awarded in the amount of $750.00 as against attorney David C. Macpherson in favor of Defendant Shauna Lea Matlin within 15 days of the service of Notice of Entry of the Order arising from this hearing. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SFS 39, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. HARRY ANDREWS et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 26, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT HARRY ANDREWS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Denied. Defendant failed to file a demurrer, as required by CRC 3.1320(a). Defendant only filed a notice of motion and memorandum of points and authority. (See Weil & Brown, Cal Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:98.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MAYA DI NAPOLI VS. FOUR SEASONS HOTELS LIMITED, A CANADIAN CORPORATIO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 26, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT ANNA GATTI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the fourth cause of action for "intentional tort - mental suffering" the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts establishing that Defendant Anna Gatti engaged in outrageous conduct, or conduct "so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized society." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 494.) As to the fifth cause of action for negligence, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint does not plead facts establishing that Ms. Gatti owed a duty to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not demonstrated how the complaint could be amended to state facts establishing a duty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
